title: spergula arvensis subsp. arvensis
intro: Spergula arvensis subsp. arvensis, also known as Corn Spurrey or Catchfly, is a perennial weed plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Spergula arvensis subsp. arvensis is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and its herbal preparations can also be found in the form of dried flowers and leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or added to herbal remedies.
